mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment directly expands aggregate income, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of PAYEMS on 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 or the proportion of instances with positive returns when PAYEMS increases month-over-month is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate from elevated levels signals a recovering labor market, which increases consumer confidence and durable consumption, lifting equity prices over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.0% after being above it for at least 2 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE triggers, the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances, or the average excess return over baseline is less than 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains at very low levels, real interest rates decline, reducing the discount rate on future earnings and raising equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
regime_hint: low rate environment with nascent inflation
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce a positive mean 20-day forward return at 95% confidence, or the Spearman rank correlation between the CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS ratio and forward returns is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime marked by high unemployment and near-zero policy rates, rising industrial production signals strong capacity utilization and economic momentum, which positively drives short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: In the specified regime, 20-day forward returns following triggers are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail and food service sales directly reflect consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive weekly equity returns as the data is absorbed by markets.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return after RSAFS releases has a Sharpe ratio below 0 in the walk-forward period, or the Spearman IC with RSAFS level is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A meaningful recovery in housing starts from a depressed level signals improving real estate markets, wealth effects, and construction employment, which boosts economic growth and quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 900 thousand units after being below 800 for at least 3 months
failure_condition: When triggered, the 60-day forward return is negative in a majority of cases, or the cumulative excess return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive surprises in both employment and industrial production provide a robust confirmation of broad-based economic expansion, which is more reliable and strongly positive for equities over a month than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% and INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a lower Sharpe ratio than either single proxy signal alone, or the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly positive at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle where the Federal Reserve is actively raising rates, an increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter financial conditions, which compress valuations and negatively affect short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points in a single month
failure_condition: In distinct tightening episodes, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time after a rate hike, or the average return is not statistically different from zero.
